target.name: Wiliot
target.founder: Tal Tamir, Yaron Elboim, Alon Yehezkely

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $230M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Wiliot is a Series C semiconductor company that has raised $230M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 8 private frontier-chip companies (including SambaNova Systems, Tenstorrent, Positron AI, and DEEPX) and 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), though the public comp method is inapplicable due to lack of disclosed revenue.

Two quantitative methods anchor the analysis. Stage-progression applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C progression, yielding $391M. The peer-median approach values Wiliot against four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.9B and a range of $529M to $5.0B. The peer set reflects meaningful heterogeneity: DEEPX at $529M (Series C, same stage) represents a conservative bound, while SambaNova and Tenstorrent at $5.0B and $2.7B respectively (both Series D) suggest upside for stage advancement.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $332M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide range reflects stage uncertainty and peer dispersion typical of frontier-chip companies. Key sensitivities include timing to Series D, commercialization traction relative to peers, and whether Wiliot tracks closer to early-stage benchmarks like DEEPX or growth-stage leaders like SambaNova. The inapplicability of public comps due to missing revenue data represents an unresolved analytical gap.
